descriptionShort: Port Coquitlam wins national junior hockey championship in overtime against Ontario visitor.
descriptionLong: Port Coquitlam (PoCo) defeated an Ontario team in overtime to win the national junior hockey championship. Steve Penner scored the overtime winner for the third consecutive game. The victory capped a successful season that included a provincial title and gold medal in the Pop Styles pre-season tournament. The article notes that opposing coaches Phillips and Dan Walton were former BCJHL rivals, with Phillips having been a member of the Centennial Cup champion Richmond Sockeyes.

attribution.headline: PoCo Wins National Championship in Overtime
